revision.text: #redirect[[en:Nakayama_Otsuka_Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。
== 概要 == 全長120メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが着くと言うことは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないか想像される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いている。出現期前方後円墳の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片<ref>大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は[[箸墓古墳]]、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は吉備地方でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト政権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。</ref>が見つかっており、箸墓古墳に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。
南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。
発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は持ち送りにしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけ壕の址がみられた。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』泉森こう（白に交）世界思想社 2004年 ISBN 4-7907-1062-9
== 関連項目 == *[[大和古墳群]] *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[日本の史跡一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 中山大塚古墳 |画像= |所在地= 天理市中山町461・494番地 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模= 全長 130m |埋葬施設= 竪穴式石室 割竹形木棺 |築造年代= 古墳時代前期初 |被葬者= |出土品= 鏡片、鉄剣破片、鉄鏃 |史跡指定= 奈良県指定（2004年） |特記事項= }} '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、奈良県の史跡に指定されている（2004年3月31日指定）。
== 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが着くと言うことは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないか想像される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いている。出現期前方後円墳の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片<ref>大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は[[箸墓古墳]]、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は吉備地方でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト政権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。</ref>が見つかっており、箸墓古墳に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。
南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。
発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は持ち送りにしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や木棺を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている<ref>泉武「大和の古墳」p.159</ref>。
墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけ壕の址がみられた。
古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』泉森皎 世界思想社 2004年 ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 『大和の古墳I』泉森皎編（人文書院、2003年）
== 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[日本の史跡一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。

墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。

[[Category:天理市]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: カラー空中写真を追加
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 中山大塚古墳 |画像=[[Image:Nakayama_ohtsuka_kohun_aerial.jpg‎|250px]] |画像キャプション=中山大塚古墳{{国土航空写真}} |所在地= 天理市中山町461・494番地 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模= 全長 130m |埋葬施設= 竪穴式石室 割竹形木棺 |築造年代= 古墳時代前期初 |被葬者= |出土品= 鏡片、鉄剣破片、鉄鏃 |史跡指定= 奈良県指定（2004年） |特記事項= }} '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、奈良県の史跡に指定されている（2004年3月31日指定）。
== 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが付くということは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないかと推定される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いており、[[出現期古墳|出現期前方後円墳]]の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片<ref>大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は箸墓古墳、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は[[吉備国|吉備地方]]でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト王権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。</ref>が見つかっており、[[箸墓古墳]]に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。
南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。
発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は持ち送りにしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や[[棺|木棺]]を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている<ref>泉森編「大和の古墳」p.159</ref>。石室のなかからは[[割竹形木棺]]が確認された。
墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。
古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[泉森皎]]『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』世界思想社、2004年。ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 泉森皎編『大和の古墳I』近畿日本鉄道、2003年。ISBN 4-409-52041-5
== 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[日本の史跡一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 中山大塚古墳 |画像=[[Image:Nakayama_ohtsuka_kohun_aerial.jpg‎|250px]] |画像キャプション=中山大塚古墳{{国土航空写真}} |所在地= 天理市中山町461・494番地 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模= 全長 130m |埋葬施設= 竪穴式石室 割竹形木棺 |築造年代= 古墳時代前期初 |被葬者= |出土品= 鏡片、鉄剣破片、鉄鏃 |史跡指定= 奈良県指定（2004年） |特記事項= }} '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、奈良県の史跡に指定されている（2004年3月31日指定）。
== 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが付くということは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないかと推定される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いており、[[出現期古墳|出現期前方後円墳]]の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片<ref>大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は箸墓古墳、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は[[吉備国|吉備地方]]でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト王権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。</ref>が見つかっており、[[箸墓古墳]]に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。
南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。
発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は[[持ち送り]]にしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や[[棺|木棺]]を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている<ref>泉森編「大和の古墳」p.159</ref>。石室のなかからは[[割竹形木棺]]が確認された。
墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。
古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[泉森皎]]『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』世界思想社、2004年。ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 泉森皎編『大和の古墳I』近畿日本鉄道、2003年。ISBN 4-409-52041-5
== 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[日本の史跡一覧]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 中山大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に後円部、左に前方部） |所属=[[萱生古墳群]]（[[大和古墳群]]） |所在地= 天理市中山町461・494番地 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模= 全長 130m |埋葬施設= 竪穴式石室 割竹形木棺 |築造年代= 古墳時代前期初 |被葬者= |出土品= 鏡片、鉄剣破片、鉄鏃 |史跡指定= 国の[[史跡]]「中山大塚古墳」
（「大和古墳群」に包含） |特記事項= }} [[File:Nakayama_ohtsuka_kohun_aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{国土航空写真}}]] '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが付くということは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないかと推定される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いており、[[出現期古墳|出現期前方後円墳]]の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は箸墓古墳、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は[[吉備国|吉備地方]]でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト王権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。が見つかっており、[[箸墓古墳]]に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。 南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。 発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は[[持ち送り]]にしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や[[棺|木棺]]を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている泉森編「大和の古墳」p.159。石室のなかからは[[割竹形木棺]]が確認された。 墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。 古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。 [[2014年]]（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[下池山古墳]]とともに「大和古墳群」として国の[[史跡]]に指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[泉森皎]]『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』世界思想社、2004年。ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 泉森皎編『大和の古墳I』近畿日本鉄道、2003年。ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大和古墳群]]

（「大和古墳群」に包含） |特記事項= }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|中山大塚古墳。2008年。}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが付くということは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないかと推定される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いており、[[出現期古墳|出現期前方後円墳]]の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は箸墓古墳、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は[[吉備国|吉備地方]]でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト王権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。が見つかっており、[[箸墓古墳]]に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。 南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。 発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52年）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は[[持ち送り]]にしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や[[棺|木棺]]を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている泉森編「大和の古墳」p.159。石室のなかからは[[割竹形木棺]]が確認された。 墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。 古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。 [[2014年]]（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[下池山古墳]]とともに「大和古墳群」として国の[[史跡]]に指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[泉森皎]]『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』世界思想社、2004年。ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 泉森皎編『大和の古墳I』近畿日本鉄道、2003年。ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大和古墳群]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 中山大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]

[[円筒埴輪|普通円筒埴輪]] |史跡指定= 国の[[史跡]]「中山大塚古墳」
（「大和古墳群」に包含） |特記事項= }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|中山大塚古墳。2008年。}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが付くということは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないかと推定される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いており、[[出現期古墳|出現期前方後円墳]]の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は箸墓古墳、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は[[吉備国|吉備地方]]でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト王権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。が見つかっており、[[箸墓古墳]]に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。 南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。 発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52年）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は[[持ち送り]]にしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や[[棺|木棺]]を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている泉森編「大和の古墳」p.159。石室のなかからは[[割竹形木棺]]が確認された。 墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。 古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。 [[2014年]]（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[下池山古墳]]とともに「大和古墳群」として国の[[史跡]]に指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[泉森皎]]『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』世界思想社、2004年。ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 泉森皎編『大和の古墳I』近畿日本鉄道、2003年。ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大和古墳群]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 中山大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に後円部、左に前方部） |所属=[[萱生古墳群]]（[[大和古墳群]]） |所在地= 天理市中山町461・494番地 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模= 全長 130m |埋葬施設= 竪穴式石室 割竹形木棺 |築造年代= [[3世紀]]後半奈良県天理市（2021）、[https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html 中山大塚古墳／天理市] |被葬者=不明
（伝）[[渟名城入姫命]] |出土品= 鏡片、鉄剣破片、鉄鏃
[[特殊器台・特殊壺|宮山型特殊器台・特殊壺・都月型特殊器台形埴輪・特殊壺形埴輪]]
[[円筒埴輪|普通円筒埴輪]] |史跡指定= 国の[[史跡]]「中山大塚古墳」
（「大和古墳群」に包含） |特記事項= }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|中山大塚古墳。2008年。}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが付くということは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないかと推定される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いており、[[出現期古墳|出現期前方後円墳]]の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は箸墓古墳、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は[[吉備国|吉備地方]]でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト王権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。が見つかっており、[[箸墓古墳]]に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。 南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。 発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52年）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は[[持ち送り]]にしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や[[棺|木棺]]を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている泉森編「大和の古墳」p.159。石室のなかからは[[割竹形木棺]]が確認された。 墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。 古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。 [[2014年]]（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[下池山古墳]]とともに「大和古墳群」として国の[[史跡]]に指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}}。 中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|なら歴史芸術文化村企画展示時に撮影。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[泉森皎]]『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』世界思想社、2004年。ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 泉森皎編『大和の古墳I』近畿日本鉄道、2003年。ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大和古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijozan|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun artifacts.jpg|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] It is a [[kofun]] in the {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} These are the group members *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}
*{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}
*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}}
*{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}}
*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Caegory:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|Category:大和古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[category:new pages]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijozan|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun artifacts.jpg|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] It is a [[kofun]] in the {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} These are the group members *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijozan|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun artifacts.jpg|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] It is a [[kofun]] in the {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} These are the group members *{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}

*{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}}

*{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}}
*{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}

== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|Category:大和古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Category]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

{{Infobox ancient site | Name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}

A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Tenri]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::大和古墳群}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Mount Miwa Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::properly attributed pages]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::History of Tenri]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:::Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003.
