{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:大和古墳群}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads

{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:大和古墳群}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]][[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:kk]][[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:大和古墳群}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]][[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:kk]][[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:大和古墳群}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]][[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:kk]][[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}

{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}}{{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == Related Topics == * [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|大和古墳群}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{draft categories| [[Category:dd]][[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamato Tumulus Group|ja|Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:大和古墳群}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]][[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:kk]][[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | Affiliation = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Dimensions = Total length: 130m | Burial_facility = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | Construction_period = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Buried_person = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} | Designation = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}}, and is designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive shape of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its form. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == Burial Facility == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was designated a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated items
