Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = [[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Overview of the tumulus (rear circular portion on the right, front rectangular portion on the left) | part_of = {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}}) | location = Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|map_size:10000}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=draft}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Special platforms and jars|1=Special platforms and jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=draft}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}}) | Additional_notes = }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|ja|櫛山古墳|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=draft}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Special platforms and jars|1=Special platforms and jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=draft}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|12=draft}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=draft}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=draft}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:萱生古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[sn:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Special platforms and jars|1=Special platforms and jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=draft}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}. == Construction == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|12=draft}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=draft}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|ja|大和神社|12=draft}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} **{{ill|Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=draft}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=draft}} **{{ill|Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=draft}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=draft}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=draft}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:萱生古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[sn:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[en:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:中山大塚古墳 up to rev 101666351
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 中山大塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に後円部、左に前方部） |所属=[[萱生古墳群]]（[[大和古墳群]]） |所在地= 天理市中山町461・494番地 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|33|57|N|135|50|53|E|scale:10000}} |形状= 前方後円墳 |規模= 全長 130m |埋葬施設= 竪穴式石室 割竹形木棺 |築造年代= [[3世紀]]後半奈良県天理市（2021）、[https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html 中山大塚古墳／天理市] |被葬者=不明
（伝）[[渟名城入姫命]] |出土品= 鏡片、鉄剣破片、鉄鏃
[[特殊器台・特殊壺|宮山型特殊器台・特殊壺・都月型特殊器台形埴輪・特殊壺形埴輪]]
[[円筒埴輪|普通円筒埴輪]] |史跡指定= 国の[[史跡]]「中山大塚古墳」
（「大和古墳群」に包含） |特記事項= }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|中山大塚古墳。2008年。}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが付くということは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないかと推定される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いており、[[出現期古墳|出現期前方後円墳]]の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は箸墓古墳、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は[[吉備国|吉備地方]]でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト王権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。が見つかっており、[[箸墓古墳]]に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。 南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。 発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52年）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は[[持ち送り]]にしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や[[棺|木棺]]を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている泉森編「大和の古墳」p.159。石室のなかからは[[割竹形木棺]]が確認された。 墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。 古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。 [[2014年]]（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[下池山古墳]]とともに「大和古墳群」として国の[[史跡]]に指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}}。 中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|なら歴史芸術文化村企画展示時に撮影。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[泉森皎]]『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』世界思想社、2004年。ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 泉森皎編『大和の古墳I』近畿日本鉄道、2003年。ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大和古墳群]]

（「大和古墳群」に包含） |特記事項= }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|中山大塚古墳。2008年。}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] '''中山大塚古墳'''（なかやまおおつかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]中山町大塚に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[大和古墳群]]の枝群である[[萱生古墳群]]に属し、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を南西に向けた前方後円墳で、全長約130メートル、後円部の径約67メートル・高さ約11.3メートル、3段築成で、後円部北側の墳丘裾に扇型に開く張り出し部分が付属することが確認された。後円部に張り出しが付くということは、すぐ南にある[[柳本古墳群]]中の代表的な[[双方中円墳]]の[[櫛山古墳]]の墳形に繋がるのではないかと推定される。前方部後方東側にも三角形の張り出しが付けられている。前方部と後円部の高さは、前方部が低く、前方部前面が緩やかに曲線を描いており、[[出現期古墳|出現期前方後円墳]]の特徴を備えている。また、後円部の張り出し付近から[[特殊器台・特殊壺|特殊器台]]の破片大和で特殊器台・特殊壺を持つ古墳は箸墓古墳、[[西殿塚古墳]]、弁天塚古墳とこの中山大塚古墳の4古墳である。特殊土器は[[吉備国|吉備地方]]でつくられていることから、吉備勢力と畿内中心勢力との政治的な結びつきによって[[ヤマト王権]]が形成されていったのではないかと推定されている。他に吉備勢力東遷説を唱える向きもある。が見つかっており、[[箸墓古墳]]に次ぐ最古級の前方後円墳であると推定されている。 南側に延びた尾根を切り取り、その土を後円部の上に積み、前方部も尾根を低く細く削り取って、形を整えたと考えられており、後円部の高さ11.3メートルのうち、墳頂部の4メートル以上は盛り土であった。しかも盛り土の仕方は、粘土と砂利を互層に堅く積み上げる工夫がなされていた。 発掘調査、[[1977年]]（昭和52年）以来数回にわたって実施されてきた。埋葬施設は、後円部の中央に長さ7.5メートル、高さ1.4メートルの[[竪穴式石室]]が築かれていた。石室の石材は、[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]麓に産出する[[輝石安山岩]]であり、上部は[[持ち送り]]にしてすぼめるなどの工夫を凝らしている。礫敷や[[棺|木棺]]を設置する基台などは確認されておらず、内部構造は比較的簡素なものとなっている泉森編「大和の古墳」p.159。石室のなかからは[[割竹形木棺]]が確認された。 墳丘の最下段に幅6メートル、高さ1メートルの平坦面が造られ、後円部の北西からくびれ部にかけて壕の跡がみられた。 古墳は[[大和神社]]の所有地内にあり、前方部の南側には墳丘の一部を削って境外摂社の御旅所坐神社（大和稚宮）が造られている。 [[2014年]]（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[下池山古墳]]とともに「大和古墳群」として国の[[史跡]]に指定された{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}}。 中山大塚古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|なら歴史芸術文化村企画展示時に撮影。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[泉森皎]]『日本考古学を学ぶ人のために』世界思想社、2004年。ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * 泉森皎編『大和の古墳I』近畿日本鉄道、2003年。ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == 関連項目 == * [[大和古墳群]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかやまおおつかこふん}} [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:大和古墳群]]

revision.text:
[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11364125]] Wikidata name: Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|qq=|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|qq=|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中山大塚古墳|version=101666351|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11364125}}

{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}

The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
