Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中山大塚古墳|version=101666351|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11364125}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364125]] Wikidata name: Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中山大塚古墳|version=101666351|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{everybodywiki link|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11364125}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads

{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads

{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中山大塚古墳|version=101666351|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{everybodywiki link|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11364125}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364125]] Wikidata name: Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]] in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Ōyamato Shrine]], with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中山大塚古墳|version=101666351|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11364125}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|WD=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun}}. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中山大塚古墳|version=101666351|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11364125}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

revision.text:
[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364125]] Wikidata name: Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to {{ill|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|en|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|WD=Q13429672|lt=Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion}} to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|WD=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun}}. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}

Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads

{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|WD=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun}}. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}

[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|WD=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun}}. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sekai Shisōsha, 2004. ISBN 4-7907-1062-9 * [[泉森皎]], ed. ''Yamato Kofun I.'' Kinki Nippon Railway, 2003. ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中山大塚古墳|version=101666351|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11364125}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakayama Otsuka Kofun (中山大塚古墳) | image = File:Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.565833|N|135.848056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | area = Total length: 130m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin | built = Late 3rd century{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1403514037345.html|title=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City|accessdate=2021}} | Interred_individual = Unknown
Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|ja|渟名城入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunakakura no Hime no Mikoto|1=Princess Nunakiirihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561867}} | Excavated_items = Fragments of mirrors, iron swords, and arrowheads
{{ill|Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama-style special pedestal and jar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}}
{{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} | designation1 = National Historic Site (as part of {{ill|Yamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) | Additional_notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:080515 Nakayama Otsuka aerial.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun, 2008}} {{Small|{{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}}]] '''Nakayama Otsuka Kofun''' (中山大塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} from the early Kofun period located in Nakayama Town, Tenri City, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It belongs to the {{ill|Kayo Kofun Cluster|ja|萱生古墳群|lt=Kayō Kofun Group|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayo Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大和古墳群}}, a branch of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, and is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Yamato Kofun Cluster. == Overview == The tumulus is oriented with the rectangular front portion facing southwest and measures approximately 130 meters in total length. The rear circular portion has a diameter of about 67 meters and a height of 11.3 meters, constructed in three tiers. A fan-shaped protrusion was identified on the northern side of the rear mound, suggesting a connection to the distinctive type of nearby {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun}} in the {{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|ja|柳本古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Additionally, a triangular protrusion is present on the eastern side of the front mound. The construction techniques reflect characteristics of the earliest keyhole-shaped tumuli, including a lower height for the front portion and gently curved front lines. Fragments of {{ill|special pedestals and jars|ja|特殊器台・特殊壺|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|1=Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|lt=Special plattypes and jars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/special pedestals and jars|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial vessel stands or jars|qq=}} were discovered near the rear mound protrusion, linking the kofun to the oldest examples of keyhole-shaped tumuli such as {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|WD=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun}}. == built == The mound's soil was sourced by cutting the southern ridge, which was shaped into the rear mound, while the front portion was carved to refine its type. Over four meters of the rear mound's height consists of artificial fill, layered with alternating clay and gravel for stability. == burial == Excavations conducted since 1977 revealed a 7.5-meter-long vertical stone chamber at the center of the rear mound. The chamber's stones were sourced from the base of {{ill|Mount Nijō|en|Mount Nijō|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and exhibit intricate construction techniques such as inward-sloping upper walls. A {{ill|split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} was confirmed within the chamber. == Surrounding Features == The lowest tier of the mound features a flat terrace about 6 meters wide and 1 meter high, with traces of a moat observed along the northwest of the rear mound and the constricted middle section. The kofun is located within the precincts of {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Ōyamato Shrine}}, with a section of the southern front mound repurposed for the shrine's outer site, housing the Mitamaya Shrine (Ōyamato Wakamiya). == Historical Significance == In 2014, Nakayama Otsuka Kofun was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site as part of the "Yamato Kofun Cluster," alongside {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikeyama Kofun|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/40100003861|title=Yamato Kofun Cluster|publisher=Historic Site Online|accessdate=2021}} File:中山大塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{Small|Photographed during a special exhibition at the Nara Historical and Cultural Village.}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[泉森皎]]. ''For Students of Japanese Archaeology.'' ISBN 4-409-52041-5 == See Also == * {{Japanese-history-stub}} * {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} **{{ill|Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikeyama Kofun|lt=Shimoikemayama Kofun|ja|下池山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoikemayama Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishitonozuka Kofun|ja|西殿塚古墳|1=Nishitonozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitonozuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamazuka Kofun|lt=Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamatsuka Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Hie Kofun|ja|ヒエ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Kofun|qq=}} **{{ill|Higashitonotsuka Kofun|ja|東殿塚古墳|1=Higashitonotsuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashitonotsuka Kofun|qq=}}
